attack_chain.description: UNC4899 identified and approached target employee via LinkedIn under the guise of a freelance software development job opportunity.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: UNC4899 targeted victim employees in both organizations by introducing themselves over social media, one over Telegram while in the other incident over LinkedIn.

attack_chain.description: Attacker engaged the victim via social media messaging with a fake job opportunity pretext to deliver malware.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Social engineering for a fake job opportunity (T1566.002, T1078.004, T1656, T1598)

attack_chain.description: Victim was tricked into downloading and executing a malicious Docker container/image on their workstation.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Download and execution of malicious Docker container (T1610, T1204.003, T1036)

attack_chain.description: Malicious Docker container was deployed on the victim's workstation, configured in privileged mode.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Docker configuration on privilege mode (T1547 / T1548)

attack_chain.description: Additional payloads (GLASSCANNON downloader, MAZEWIRE backdoor) were downloaded onto the victim's host.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Download and execution of additional payloads onto the victim's host (T1204.002, 1204.003, T1105)

attack_chain.description: GLASSCANNON malicious Python script downloader executed on the victim host.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: MAZEWIRE (T1543.002) / GLASS-CANNON (T1059.006)

attack_chain.technique_name: Create or Modify System Process: Systemd Service
attack_chain.description: MAZEWIRE backdoor established persistence on the victim's workstation.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: MAZEWIRE (T1543.002)

attack_chain.description: GLASSCANNON and Mythic C2 communicated with actor-controlled C2 over web protocols; MAZEWIRE used proxying.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: MAZEWIRE (T1090) / GLASSCANNON (T1071.001) / Mythic C2 (T1573/T1071.001)

attack_chain.description: Attacker enumerated files and folders on the victim workstation, including credential files.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Enumeration of files, folders, including credentials (AWS long-term access key) from victim's workstation (T1592, T1589.001, T1083, T1552.001)

attack_chain.description: Retrieved AWS long-term access keys from the AWS credential file stored on the victim's host.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: long-term access keys retrieved from an AWS credential file found in the victim's host

attack_chain.description: Used stolen AWS long-term access keys to remotely interact with AWS CLI, though access was limited by IAM policies requiring STS/MFA.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: UNC4899 initially used long-term access keys retrieved from an AWS credential file found in the victim's host to remotely interact with AWS CLI.

attack_chain.description: Blocked by IAM/MFA policies, UNC4899 exfiltrated the user's STS session cookies from the workstation.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: evidence indicates that UNC4899 likely resorted to exfiltrating the user's session cookies instead.

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Web Session Cookie
attack_chain.description: Used stolen STS session cookies to authenticate to AWS, bypassing MFA restrictions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Used stolen STS session cookies to access AWS environment bypassing MFA restrictions (T1539, T1078.004)

attack_chain.description: Enumerated AWS resources and configurations including CloudFront distributions and S3 buckets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Enumeration of AWS resources, configuration, etc. (T1580, T1526, T1619)

attack_chain.description: Identified S3 bucket objects (JavaScript files) relevant to cryptocurrency functionality.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: identified relevant CloudFront configurations and S3 buckets to target

attack_chain.description: Enumerated the EKS environment via Kubernetes API.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Enumeration of EKS environment via Kubernetes API (T1613)

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Web Session Cookie
attack_chain.description: Used stolen session cookies to laterally move across production and development AWS environments, and to access Slack workspace.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Used stolen STS session cookies to gain access across production and development AWS environments (T1550.004)

attack_chain.description: Executed remote code on EKS pods via the Kubernetes API.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Remote code execution on EKS pods via Kubernetes API (T1509.009, T1609)

attack_chain.description: Attempted registration of attacker-controlled MFA devices (blocked by policy requiring existing MFA for enrollment).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Registration of MFA devices (T1098.005)

attack_chain.description: Uploaded and replaced existing JavaScript files in target S3 with malicious versions designed to manipulate cryptocurrency functions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Uploaded malicious version of JavaScript files on target S3 (T1195.001 / 1565.001)

attack_chain.technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_chain.description: Malicious JS served via CloudFront/S3 compromised the cryptocurrency application supply chain, triggering a fraudulent transaction from the target wallet.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: designed to manipulate cryptocurrency functions and trigger a transaction with the cryptocurrency wallet of a target organization

attack_chain.description: Successfully stole several million dollars worth of cryptocurrency.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: UNC4899 successfully stole several million dollars worth of cryptocurrency

attack_chain.description: Cleanup: restored benign JavaScript files, logged out of Slack, deleted backdoor from victim workstation.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Cleanup (restored benign version of the JavaScript files, logged out of Slack, deleted backdoor from victim workstation) (T1070, T1070.004, T1070.009)

initial_access_vector: Social engineering on LinkedIn posing as a freelance software development job opportunity, convincing the victim to execute a malicious Docker container on their workstation, leading to GLASSCANNON/MAZEWIRE malware deployment.
persistence_mechanism: MAZEWIRE backdoor installed as systemd service on victim workstation; continuous renewal of stolen STS session cookies from the victim's workstation after expiration.
